Movie: Thandavam || Music: GV Prakash Kumar

Adhigaalai pookal unaipaarka yaengum
Anthimaalai megam unnaipaarthe thoongum

Kanavugal tharugiraai kavithaigal tharugiraai
Uravugal tharugiraai uyirilae

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

Who is the lead actor in the movie Thandavam?

The lead actor in the movie Thandavam is Vikram.

Who composed the music for the song Adhigaalai Pookal?

The music for the song Adhigaalai Pookal was composed by G.V. Prakash Kumar.

What is the theme of the song Adhigaalai Pookal?

The song Adhigaalai Pookal portrays themes of love and longing, creating a soothing and romantic atmosphere.

When was the movie Thandavam released?

The movie Thandavam was released in the year 2012.

Who wrote the lyrics for the song Adhigaalai Pookal?

The lyrics for the song Adhigaalai Pookal were penned by the talented lyricist Na. Muthukumar.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song "Adhigaalai Pookal" from the 2012 Tamil movie "Thandavam" is a melodious track that beautifully captures the essence of love and longing. Sung and composed by G.V. Prakash Kumar, with lyrics by Na. Muthukumar, the song features the lead pair, Vikram and Anushka Shetty, in a poignant and romantic setting. The soothing melody and heartfelt lyrics evoke deep emotions and resonate with listeners. This song is a testament to the artistic collaboration between the composer, lyricist, and performers, making it a memorable piece in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Adhigaalai Pookal, a mesmerizing melody from the movie Thandavam, showcases the musical brilliance of G.V. Prakash Kumar combined with the poetic lyrics of Na. Muthukumar. Sung soulfully by G.V. Prakash Kumar himself, this song captures the essence of love and longing. Dive into this enchanting composition that resonates with emotions and cinematic beauty.
stanzaMeanings: Song: Adhigaalai Pookal || Singer: G.V.Prakash || Lyrics: Na.Muthukumar Movie: Thandavam || Music: GV Prakash Kumar
Morning flowers look for you everywhere; Evening clouds gaze at you and fall asleep.
Your eyes search for stars; Living with you is a blessing, isn't it?
You give dreams, you give poetry; You give relationships, oh life.
